channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Super Bowl returning to the Bay Area for 2026
item.description: (KRON) -- The Super Bowl is coming back to the Bay Area next year. A decade ago, the region hosted Super Bowl 50. Next year, it will play host to Super Bowl LX. On Monday morning, San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan was in New Orleans for the official Super Bowl handoff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: NFL offseason priorities include health and safety, kickoff evaluation and expanding replay assist
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The NFL offseason won’t last long. By the time the Philadelphia Eagles complete their Super Bowl victory parade on Friday, it will be less than two weeks until the scouting combine kicks off in Indianapolis. Free agency follows on March 10 with the two-day negotiating period.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warriors legend Rick Barry remembers when all-star games mattered
item.description: The last Warriors players to participate in the NBA All-Star game were Rick Barry and Nate Thurmond in 1967. Barry was MVP, but thought Thurmond should have won it.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Jason Kelce and look-alike posse take on New Orleans
item.description: Five of the most passionate NFL fan duos in America were challenged to be the first team to find the real Jason Kelce.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: MLB owners vote to install John Seidler as control person of San Diego Padres
item.description: Baseball owners voted Thursday to install John Seidler, the oldest brother of the late former Padres chairman Peter Seidler, as the team’s control person.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Virginia Halas McCaskey, longtime Bears owner and matriarch, dies at 102, team announces
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — Virginia Halas McCaskey, the longtime owner and matriarch of the Chicago Bears, died Thursday at 102, the team announced in a news release. Virginia served as the owner of the Bears since her father and team founder George S. Halas died on Oct. 31, 1983.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Three UPenn teammates of Lia Thomas sue university
item.description: Three former teammates of transgender swimmer Lia Thomas sued the University of Pennsylvania over rights violations
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Madsen brothers lead Utes past Colorado, 72-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Gabe Madsen scored 17 points, Lawson Lovering scored 12 points and Utah kept Colorado winless in the Big 12 beating the Buffaloes 72-59 on Wednesday night. Ezra Ausar and reserve Mason Madsen each scored 10 points for the Utes (13-9, 5-6 Big 12).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Bulls, Ball agree to contract extension
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bulls and point guard Lonzo Ball have agreed to a two-year contract extension, according to ESPN's Shams Charania. Charania reports the deal is worth $20 million over two years.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Local football player overcomes hardships, signs to play Division I football
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Mental toughness isn't something you're born with; it's a learned skill. One that Calvary Christian senior Nelson Sparrow Jr. has already mastered. "It feels great," he said. "I dreamt of this. I worked hard for this every single day of my life.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bucs hire former USF head coach Charlie Strong as defensive line coach
item.description: According to NFL Network's Ian Rapoport, the Bucs have hired former USF head coach Charlie Strong to be their new defensive line coach.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Matt Nagy offers advice for Ben Johnson, prepares for 2nd matchup against Vic Fangio in the Big Game
item.description: Matt Nagy, Kansas City Chiefs offensive coordinator, shared advice for the next head coach of the Chicago Bears, emphasizing the importance of winning and beating the Packers.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Tiger Woods' mother, Kultida Woods, has died
item.description: Kultida Woods, the Thai-born mother of Tiger Woods, died Tuesday morning, a week after being seen attending his indoor TMRW Golf League in Florida.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON AP Golf Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cooper Kupp says the Rams intend to trade him. He's disappointed to end his 8 years with the team
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Receiver Cooper Kupp says the Los Angeles Rams are attempting to trade him after eight seasons with the club, and he doesn't like the decision.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How GJ's Andrew Henderson became the Tigers' hero on Friday
item.description: “One of the coolest moments of my high school career," said Andrew Henderson smiling from ear to ear as he reflected on the shot of his life.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Rumors swirl about Warriors re-acquiring Kevin Durant amid chaotic NBA trade deadline
item.description: (KRON) -- The 2025 NBA trade deadline has provided even no shortage of excitement so far, and the Golden State Warriors could be the next team to get involved.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Caleb Williams voted GQ's most stylish football player
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Chicago Bears quarterback Caleb Williams has earned his first accolade since joining the NFL as the league's No. 1 overall pick last April. He took home GQ's fan vote for the Most Stylish Football Player Monday.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: MLB ump fired amid gambling probe
item.description: Major League Baseball has fired an umpire as part of an investigation into sports gambling. Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball, according to ESPN reporter Jesse Rogers and later confirmed by the league.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5
